summary.intent: Implement Task #286 — the framework-agnostic Table state machine (agnostic core) for the ars-ui component library, covering selection, sort, expansion, grid focus, column resize, and virtual-scroll ARIA metadata.
summary.outcome: Full implementation delivered across mod.rs (~1843 LOC), tests.rs (133+ tests, 37 snapshot fixtures), spec-conformance, and proptest invariants. Seven rounds of Codex review were completed with 30+ findings addressed; PR #651 is open and awaiting the pass-8 verdict.

repo: Tabs (`crates/ars-components/src/navigation/tabs/`) is the canonical structural template for complex state machines — Machine impl, TransitionPlan::context_only, ConnectApi, Part enum, Bindable controlled/uncontrolled, and SyncProps all follow its patterns.
`ars_core::NoEffect` is used when the machine produces no DOM effects; adapters read `ctx.focused_cell`/`focused_row`/`focused_col` and apply focus directly, keeping the agnostic core DOM-free.
`selection::State::toggle` requires a `Collection` generic the Table machine doesn't own — toggle must be computed locally via `is_selected + select/deselect`.
`Bindable::set()` on a controlled Bindable does NOT change what `get()` returns; `selection_state.selected_keys` must always be re-synced from `get()` after any `set()` or `sync_controlled()` call.

`Set::All` with `SelectAllMode::AllData` must be preserved on individual deselect — the adapter owns exclusion tracking via §5.2 BulkSelection. Only `AllVisible` materializes to `Multiple(ctx.rows)` on deselect/toggle.
`Direction::Auto` must never be stored in `ctx.dir`; it must be resolved to Ltr/Rtl via `ctx.locale.direction()` at `init` and in the `SetDirection` transition.
Non-finite widths (NaN, ±∞) must be rejected in the `ColumnResize` transition guard before clamping to `min_column_width`.
`Part::ColumnHeader` needs a `sortable: bool` field so the `ConnectApi::part_attrs` dispatcher can honor caller intent rather than hardcoding sortable=true.
The `post-implementation-audit` skill is mandatory before user review per CLAUDE.md step 7; the `waiting-for-codex-review` skill and Codex inline-reply+resolve loop is mandatory per step 12.
`cargo xci-fast` is the pre-push gate (10 sequential checks); `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` must pass so no orphaned snapshot fixtures remain.
Spec drifts discovered during implementation must be fixed in the same PR — the CLAUDE.md rule is 'resolve spec/code mismatch in the same task'.
Crate-level lcov line coverage is the governing threshold (≥90%), not file-level branch coverage — file-level can dip below 90 as long as the crate aggregate stays above.

`SyncControlledSortDescriptor` wraps its payload as `Option<Option<SortDescriptor<String>>>` — outer `None` exits controlled mode, inner `None` clears the sort; this differs from the other sync events.

code.finding: New file: complete Table agnostic core — State, Event (30+ variants), Context (20+ fields), Props, Messages, Machine impl (init/transition/connect), Part enum with ComponentPart derive, and Api with all *_attrs and keyboard helpers.
New file: 133 unit+snapshot tests including all 46 named tests from the issue, 7 Codex-pass-1 regression guards, 6 pass-2, 2 pass-3, 10 pass-4, 5 pass-5, 2 pass-6, and 6 pass-7 regression guards.

New file: `table_anatomy_matches_spec` asserts all 15 Part variants map to correct `data-ars-part` strings via `assert_anatomy`. Needed `Part::ColumnHeader { sortable: bool, header: String }` after Codex finding Td6C.
Extended with `proptest_table_event_sequences_preserve_invariants` (1000 cases, #[ignore]): asserts selection ⊆ enabled rows, expansion ⊆ rows, column_widths ≥ min, focus in bounds when virtualizing.

11 spec drifts resolved in the same PR: missing Context fields, FocusCell row-index hard-coded to 0, build_sort_announcement referencing undeclared locale and wrong field name, Scrolling state mention, DeselectAll/ToggleRow missing disallow_empty_selection guards, SyncControlledSortDescriptor payload type mismatch, SetIsSorting not in events table.

workflow: TDD was enforced throughout: write failing tests (red), implement fix, verify green, then run xci-fast before every push.

Codex review requires: post inline reply to each thread citing commit SHA and the regression test that locks the fix, resolve the thread via GraphQL, re-trigger `@codex review`, then restart the background poller.

Background polling scripts are fragile — they can be wiped between sessions; always re-verify PR state directly with `gh pr view` when a poller notification arrives with a failed/unexpected exit code.

Rebasing against main before force-pushing is required; merge conflicts in `spec_conformance.rs` (main added `layout` module while branch added `data_display`) must be resolved by keeping both module registrations.
Snapshot fixtures must be accepted with `cargo insta accept` after a test run creates them; running with `--unreferenced=reject` guards against orphaned fixtures.
Coverage threshold is checked at crate level, not file level — file-level branch coverage can sag as long as the crate-level lcov line total stays above 90%.
When a `socket connection was closed unexpectedly` API error interrupts a plan write, the plan write must be re-executed immediately before exiting plan mode.

friction: API socket error during plan writing (between research and write phases) caused a gap in the session that required the user to prompt 'Where is the plan?' before proceeding.
Background polling scripts (bash `until` loops checking `gh pr view`) were wiped between session restarts, requiring re-creation from scratch each time — exit code 127 on script not found.
`selection::State::toggle` requires a `Collection` generic the Table machine doesn't own, forcing manual toggle computation via `is_selected + select/deselect` rather than delegating to the library.
Seven rounds of Codex review with 30+ findings — primarily around controlled-Bindable semantics (`set()` vs `get()` invariant), `Set::All` materialization semantics, and `Direction::Auto` resolution.
Clippy warnings (unused `mut`, path qualification, collapsible `if`) blocked `cargo xci-fast` on multiple passes, requiring iterative fix-and-rerun cycles.
File-level branch coverage near the 90% threshold required adding targeted coverage-backfill tests on two separate occasions to stay above the gate.
Rebase conflicts in `crates/ars-components/tests/spec_conformance.rs` required manual merge (main added `layout`, branch added `data_display`).
`Part::ColumnResizeHandle { column: String }` required careful handling in the `part_attrs` dispatcher because the current-width must be looked up from `ctx.column_widths` rather than hardcoded.
open_items: `ScrollToRow(Key)` event and full `ars-collections::Virtualizer` integration deferred to a follow-up issue — requires widening `type Effect` from `NoEffect` to a typed enum.
`BulkSelection` exclusion-set wrapper (§5.2) is adapter/application code; not implemented in the agnostic core. The core preserves `Set::All` for `AllData` mode; adapter owns exclusion tracking.
`on_load_more: Option<Callback<()>>` callback is adapter-only and was explicitly not stored in the core.

Codex pass 7 was addressed (4 findings: mode-tighten selection normalization, NaN width guard, resize-handle width lookup, `select_all_mode` demotion in SyncProps), committed, pushed, and replied — but pass 8 had not yet been triggered at session end.
The `snapshot-reviewed` PR label must be re-confirmed present since each push with new snapshot fixtures requires it per CLAUDE.md.
